scope_boundary: Explicit v1.0 no-promotion decisions for manifest chapter core claims that do not yet have accepted evidence-transition records. These decisions keep chapter core support states at argument and do not promote any ASI capability, runtime behavior, benchmark, safety, deployment, source-interpretation, or model-quality claim.
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent external audit
global_promotion_burden: Move a chapter core claim above argument only through an accepted evidence-transition record that names the narrowed claim, reviewed artifacts, commands where relevant, limitations, counterevidence, non-claims, and support-state effect.

decisions.chapter_title: ASI Is a Stack, Not a Model
decisions.decision_reason: The chapter has source mappings and finite stack-boundary Lean hooks, but no end-to-end stack implementation, deployed handoff trace, integration benchmark, or independent architecture review has been accepted as evidence for the broad stack claim.
required_evidence: end-to-end governed stack trace
layer handoff artifacts with authority receipts
accepted transition for a narrowed stack-mechanics claim
blockers: no deployed or replayed stack run
finite proofs do not establish whole-stack behavior
no independent architecture review
non_claims: does not prove ASI is implemented
does not prove stack architecture outperforms model-centric alternatives

replayed failure traces or incident mapping

measured localized-repair and direct-generation comparison

does not prove safety, support, readiness, release, transfer, SOTA, AGI, or ASI

decisions.chapter_title: Governed Operations, Incident Command, and Graceful Degradation
decisions.decision_reason: The chapter has source-reviewed lifecycle and incident guidance plus an effect-complete operations design, but no local fault-injected service, adversarial game-day matrix, full-state rollback and compensation result, real incident, independent reproduction, or transfer has been accepted for its broad claim.
required_evidence: fault-injected reference service covering model, optimizer, scheduler, RNG, cache, memory, data, credentials, replicas, descendants, backups, and external effects
compound, correlated, ambiguous, and detector-degrading game days with strong SRE baselines and joint harm, useful-throughput, latency, operator-load, and governance-cost accounting
independent recovery exercise and transfer with expiring emergency authority and complete residual disposition
blockers: no local effect-complete rollback and compensation harness covering descendants and external effects
no adversarial game-day evidence calibrates detection, severity, containment, or recovery thresholds
general incident-response guidance has not been independently validated for autonomous AI
non_claims: does not prove detector quality, rollback completeness, effect reversibility, or recovery safety
does not prove resilience from monitoring, backups, a kill switch, an incident plan, or a successful code restore
does not prove bounded harm, readiness, safe deployment, or ASI

decisions.chapter_title: Privacy, Data Rights, and Information-Flow Governance
decisions.decision_reason: The chapter has a source-reviewed argument packet, authored lifecycle record, rejecting mutations, finite proofs, and an unopened campaign, but no personal-data processing, natural privacy mechanism, attack, rights workflow, legal determination, independent reproduction, or transfer has been accepted for its broad claim.
required_evidence: competent natural six-arm campaign with positive-controlled extraction and membership attacks
complete derivative rights workflow with separate storage behavioral influence privacy and legal outcomes
independent reproduction and transfer within the exact threat model privacy unit mechanism and jurisdictional nonclaim envelope
blockers: natural campaign unexecuted
no local privacy mechanism or strong attack result
no legal applicability or compliance determination
no independent reproduction or transfer
non_claims: does not establish valid consent lawful basis or legal compliance
does not establish a privacy guarantee attack absence complete lineage total erasure forgetting or influence removal
does not establish support readiness release transfer AGI ASI or SOTA
